name: Layer-1 meta-labeling secondary model (XGBoost win-filter on the carry edge)
revision: Revised from fixed-horizon binary direction to META-LABELING (Lopez de Prado). The original label (fwd_direction_5d = sign(close[t+5]-close[t])) trained the model to predict drift, not whether a carry signal produces a winning trade under the exit rules. Supersedes the prior hash 8cea71c9d3c29f8eda88a299f02a61a502237161f44bc16537a2be0fc623367c. [intra-revision re-hash: ablation clarified UNCONDITIONAL; prior meta-label hash 1cb3b918... superseded.] [barriers corrected to strict_mode=False reality: Donchian INACTIVE in the canonical run; stop is ATR-based; pnl is net-of-costs. prior hash f82d22af... superseded.]
phase: Phase-2 (data) revised. NO model trained; holdout 2024+ untouched.
purpose: Build a META-LABELING SECONDARY MODEL: the carry edge (primary) fixes direction; XGBoost (secondary) predicts P(win) for each carry signal. The model never sets direction or size by itself — it gates/weights carry signals. Formal framing: Lopez de Prado meta-labeling (primary decides side, secondary decides whether to act); it is the academic name for the two-layer propose/dispose split.
mechanism: Primary = sovereign/forex/signal_engine.build_signal_frame (carry/IRP/RRD events). Secondary = XGBoost over the 41-feature panel (docs/layer1/feature_windows.json) evaluated at each signal's ENTRY bar -> P(this trade wins). A setup fires only when P(win) >= a tuned threshold.
prior_expectation: NOT_SIGNIFICANT. Daily forex features were flat in discovery (28->0); Sovereign Core ML died p=0.164. Meta-labeling can still help by IMPROVING PRECISION (filtering carry's losers) even where direction is unpredictable — that is the one genuinely-open question this tests. Still a falsification test: a clean kill is the expected, valuable outcome.
model_design: Meta-labeling secondary model. Trained on SIGNAL EVENTS ONLY (one row per carry trade), NOT the full daily panel. Pooled across the 5 pairs. Direction is fixed by the primary; the secondary never predicts direction (that is what makes meta-labeling overfit-resistant).

label.name: meta_win
label.definition: 1 if the carry trade's realized NET pnl_pct > 0 (after per-pair spread+swap costs) else 0, under the canonical exit machine.

barriers.strict_mode: False — CANONICAL/PROVEN config (prove.py:50 instantiates ForexBacktester(start,end) at the default strict_mode=False; this is the Sharpe-1.25 exit machine L2 will port).
barriers.stop_status: ACTIVE — ATR stop (stop_atr_mult>0 so STOP_PCT fallback is NOT used)
barriers.time_status: ACTIVE (fires only when not strict_mode — fast_backtester.py:107)
barriers.donchian_status: CONFIGURED BUT INACTIVE in strict_mode=False — fires only in strict_mode AND for longs (fast_backtester.py:100). CORRECTED 2026-06-26 after reading the code (operator confirmation #1); the earlier pre-reg listed it as active.
barriers.take_profit: NONE — 'win' = realized NET pnl>0, the disposer's actual verdict (NNUE), not an idealized +2ATR TP.
barriers.costs: net of per-pair spread + swap via forex_backtester._apply_costs

sampling: One row per carry trade (signal != 0) with a realized exit. Open-at-boundary trades dropped.
feature_spec: docs/layer1/feature_windows.json (41 features built; 10 deferred per data/layer1/load_report.json)
feature_source: data/layer1/features_v1.parquet, joined at each trade's entry_date+pair
holdout_discipline: TOUCH ONCE, after both bars are evaluated on validation. Not read in Phase 2.
model_class: XGBoost binary classifier — reuse sovereign/ml_trainer.py (purged walk-forward, uniqueness weights, isotonic calibration).
config_cap_rationale: 3 x 2 x 2 = 12 configs (label is now exit-defined, no horizon sweep). BH across all 12.

validation_protocol.purged_cv: walk_forward_validation.py:88-138 (purge = max feature lookback + max hold; embargo after test).
validation_protocol.multiple_comparisons: gate.py benjamini_hochberg across all 12 configs; deflated metrics.
validation_protocol.meta_gate_test: gate.py bootstrap_sharpe_diff_pvalue: meta-gated carry vs carry-alone OOS.

success_criteria.statistical: validation ROC-AUC >= 0.55 AND beats the directional permutation null at p<0.05 AFTER Benjamini-Hochberg across all 12 configs AND deflated metric > 0.
success_criteria.economic: the meta-gate (take a carry trade only when P(win) >= tuned threshold) beats carry-alone on OOS mean-R by >= +0.10R per trade WHILE retaining >= 50% of carry trades (HYP-044 selectivity trap), bootstrap Sharpe-diff p<0.05, positive in walk-forward.

feature_independence_ablation: ALWAYS-RUN (unconditional): the ablation is a standard step of the Phase-3 analysis pipeline, executed regardless of the main model's outcome — NOT a post-hoc check run only if results look surprising. Retrain WITHOUT {day_of_week, pair_real_rate_diff, pair_rate_diff_mom_1m, pair_rate_diff_mom_3m, pair_rate_diff_mom_6m} (these overlap live hypotheses HYP-050 DOW veto, HYP-052c & HYP-054 real-rate-diff gates). If OOS Sharpe drops <5% when ablated, those features are REDUNDANT with the carry edge (not independent alpha) and their importance is not credited.
failure_criteria: Fails EITHER bar -> NOT_SIGNIFICANT. Isolation test ever fails -> INVALID_METHODOLOGY.
label_caveat_option_B: Labels are from the SIMULATED exit machine (_simulate_forex_core), not a live L2. This is the exact exit logic L2 will port, so the gap is sim-vs-live execution fidelity only. MANDATORY retrain + re-validation once L2 is live and has accumulated real exit outcomes (the NNUE point).
tautology_guard: tests/test_feature_label_isolation.py must pass on the real meta_dataset_v1 before any training.
no_mid_run_tuning: Grid + config_cap + splits + label + barriers frozen here; the trainer asserts a match before training.
isolation: Phase 2 writes docs/layer1/*, sovereign/layer1/*, tests/*, data/layer1/*, this pre-reg. No live config, no Layer 2 touch, no holdout read, no commit without approval.
hash_method: sha256 of json.dumps(body, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) where body = this object MINUS the hash_lock field
